While generics will remain tariff-free for two years, duties will rise to 100% from August 2028 and 200% from August 2029, weighing on investor sentiment.

items.title: BPCL Q1 Results Live Updates: BPCL reports loss of Rs 3,962 crore; revenue rises 23% YoY. Eternal, HPCL and more to report earnings
Jaiprakash Power Ventures among 4 stocks showing bullish RSI upswing
items.summary: Four stocks in the Nifty500 pack that gained over 3% featured in the RSI Trending Up scan on July 21 after showing improving momentum. The RSI moved above the 50 mark from lower levels, signalling strengthening price action, improving sentiment and potential short-term buying opportunities for traders tracking bullish technical setups.

items.title: India weighs letting airport owners Adani, GMR run own airlines to curb duopoly
items.summary: India is discussing a policy shift to permit airport operators to own airlines. This change could allow Adani Group and GMR Airports to establish their own carriers. Current rules restrict airport operators' airline ownership stakes significantly. The move aims to increase competition against dominant carriers like IndiGo and Air India.

items.title: Sensex, Nifty likely to open lower as oil jumps on widening US-Iran war
items.summary: GIFT Nifty futures ⁠were trading at 24,117 as of 7:56 am, indicating the benchmark Nifty 50 could open below Tuesday’s close of 24,187.7.

items.title: Modi’s protectionism push creates stock winners in laggard India
items.summary: A Bloomberg-compiled basket of 15 stocks linked to import substitution has rallied more than 20% since the start of the Middle East conflict, outperforming the benchmark NSE Nifty 50 Index, which has dropped about 2%. The rally stands out as the basket has become a haven for investors seeking shelter from broader market volatility.

items.title: Sensex Today | Nifty 50 | Stock Market Live Updates: Sensex slumps over 750 pts, Nifty below 24,000; PSU banks, pharma stocks struggle
Global wars are delaying FTA talks, accounting for one-fifth of India’s trade
items.summary: These deals include separate FTAs with the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) countries, Israel, and the Russia-led Eurasian Economic Union — cumulatively accounting for about 20% of India’s total trade.

items.title: India’s scheme to attract dollar deposits from abroad is fine—but pardon local rupee savers their envy
items.summary: With inflows of over $17 billion already, India’s deposit scheme for overseas Indians is drawing foreign exchange through leveraged packages offered by banks. Savers at home do not grudge such deals but let’s spare a thought for their finances as well.

items.title: Positive Breakout: These 8 stocks cross above their 200 DMAs
items.summary: As long as the stock is priced above the 200-day SMA on the daily time frame, it is generally considered to be an overall uptrend.

items.title: Why these 10 stocks will be in focus on Tuesday
items.summary: TAFE, CDSL, SPML Infra, Emcure Pharma, Redington, Wanbury, RITES, KCP Sugar, Mafatlal and Monarch Surveyors

items.title: 8 stocks including Pharma majors will remain in focus on Wednesday following Trump’s tariff announcement
items.summary: Maruti Suzuki’s price hike, Anant Raj demerger, Aurobindo Pharma getting nod for bio similar, Anupam Rasayn open offer to Bliss GVS shareholders

items.title: Mint Quick Edit | Burnham in the hot seat: can the UK's new leader revive a Brexit-hit economy?
items.summary: The revolving door at 10 Downing Street reflects popular discontent in the UK. While the country’s new PM is given to leftist rhetoric, his best shot at success may be to embrace Labour’s centrist ‘third way’ policies.

items.title: Sebi directs depositories to freeze promoter holdings during buyback period
items.summary: Sebi has directed depositories to freeze promoter holdings during company buyback periods.  This restriction will be in effect from board approval until the offer closes.  Promoters can still participate in buybacks through the tender offer route.  Existing pledges created before the buyback period can also be invoked.  Depositories must implement these operational systems by August 1.

items.title: Stocks in news: IndusInd Bank, Adani Green, Eternal, Maruti Suzuki, Bandhan Bank
items.summary: Indian markets closed marginally higher after balancing mixed global cues and positive earnings. Adani Energy Solutions reported significant profit growth, while Adani Total Gas saw a decline. Maruti Suzuki announced a price increase, and several companies will report quarterly results soon. Bandhan Bank and Indian Hotels also posted strong first-quarter financial performances. Investors are watching key heavyweight sectors and upcoming company announcements closely.

items.title: D-St set for a negative opening as GIFT Nifty signals weak start
items.summary: Domestic equities extended losses for a second consecutive session on Tuesday.  Higher crude oil prices and geopolitical tensions pressured the market significantly.  Analysts expect equities to remain range-bound amid ongoing global uncertainties.  The ongoing Q1 earnings season will drive stock-specific actions and investor sentiment.  The Indian rupee firmed slightly as diplomacy hopes eased oil price concerns.

items.title: Trump announces new generic drugs tariff plan: No tariffs for 2 years, then 100%, and then 200%
items.summary: For Indian exporters, the two-year tariff-free period offers additional time to reassess supply chains and investment plans. While the immediate impact on exports is likely to be limited, the proposed escalation to 100% and eventually 200% tariffs could significantly alter the economics of supplying generic medicines to the US if manufacturers do not establish a stronger local production footprint.

items.title: SBI Funds' ESOPs mint fortunes for top executives
items.summary: SBI Funds Management executives received significant value from employee stock options after the company's listing. Joint CEO Devinder Pal Singh holds shares and outstanding options worth over ₹160 crore. Chief Investment Officer Srinivasan Rama Iyer's stake and ESOPs together exceed ₹165 crore. Other senior officials also saw substantial gains from their allotted employee stock options.

items.title: Months after  ₹6k crore IPO, Raajmarg Infra mulls another share sale
items.summary: The trust, which is backed by the government, is considering a qualified institutional placement or a follow-on public offer, the people said, asking not to be named because the discussions are private.

items.title: India's coking coal import bill down 39% in three years helped by Russian discounts, low-price global cycle
items.summary: India's coking coal import bill decreased from $19.2 billion in fiscal 2023 to $11.76 billion in FY26, as the country shifted imports from Australia to Russia and the US. Domestic output remains insufficient, which means domestic steelmakers are still exposed to global commodity price cycles.

items.title: RBI plans unified code for foreign investment in equity instruments
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India proposed new rules for overseas fund flows. These draft norms will replace existing regulations governing foreign investment. The framework clarifies eligible entities and defines foreign-controlled entities clearly. It also retains the distinction between direct and portfolio foreign investments. The proposed rules aim to streamline foreign investment into Indian markets.

items.title: FPIs stay in buying mode, consumer services lead sectoral inflows in first half of July
items.summary: Overseas investors became net buyers in the first half of July, marking a strong inflow period. Consumer services, metals, and healthcare sectors attracted the most foreign investment. IT sector saw marginal buying after months of consistent selling by foreign investors. Banks also received significant inflows, continuing their positive trend from June.

items.title: Indian conglomerates' new pitch to foreign investors: Build with us from day one
items.summary: Instead of waiting to sell stakes after projects are de-risked, India's largest business groups are increasingly bringing in foreign equity partners at the outset to share risk, reduce capital outlay and fund simultaneous expansion across sectors.

items.title: SBI Funds makes strong D-St debut, ends 6.3% above IPO price
items.summary: SBI Funds Management debuted on the stock market at a premium price.  The company raised over ₹9,813 crore through its initial public offering.  SBI plans no further stake dilution in the asset management company.  The fund house is investing in new Alternative Investment Funds capabilities.  SBI Funds aims to expand its reach into B30 markets.

items.title: Trade all at sea: Freight rattled by delays, ship shortages and port congestion as West Asia conflict flares up again
items.summary: Renewed conflict in West Asia is significantly disrupting India's exports, with exporters facing container shortages and limited vessel availability. Sea freight rates have surged sharply, doubling or even tripling on several routes. Congestion at key Indian ports, including Mundra and JNPT, has further worsened shipping delays. The disruption has increased logistics costs and delayed critical export shipments.

items.title: Anant Raj Board approves demerger of Data Centre & Cloud Services business
items.summary: Under the proposed arrangement, Anant Raj will first consolidate all its data centre and cloud operations under one entity before carving them out into Ashok Cloud Pvt. Ltd, a dedicated digital infrastructure and cloud services company that will be listed independently

items.title: Rs 75,000 cr OMC loss: Oil ministry working on plan to offset losses. Companies seek financial support; Finance Ministry nod needed before proposal goes to Cabinet
items.summary: Oil Ministry is working on a proposal to compensate state-run oil marketing companies for losses incurred from selling fuel below market rates during the April–June quarter. Hopes of recovering these losses have diminished as crude oil prices have risen again. The government now faces a difficult decision on compensation amid uncollected taxes. Inter-ministerial discussions are expected to take several weeks or even months before a final decision is reached.

items.title: New trade pact a win-win for India and U.K.
items.summary: The CETA can reshape trade and investment flows, create quality jobs and growth, and transition India to a more competitive and innovation-driven economy

items.title: KKR-backed Serentica Renewables lands massive $1.19B despite project delays and debt refinancing
items.summary: Serentica Renewables has secured over $1.19 billion across three financing transactions. This funding will accelerate capacity expansion and support delayed renewable energy projects. A $345 million loan comes from the Asian Development Bank and other lenders. MUFG underwrote a $397 million green term loan now being syndicated. The company aims to supply over 50 billion units of clean energy annually.

items.title: KKR-backed Serentica Renewables lands massive $1.19B despite project delays and debt refinancing
items.summary: Serentica Renewables has secured over $1.19 billion across three financing transactions. This funding will accelerate capacity expansion and support delayed renewable energy projects. items.title: ICMR licenses cancer, vaccine technologies to Emcure, Biological E
items.summary: The Indian Council of Medical Research has licensed three new biomedical technologies. These innovations aim to advance cancer treatment and infectious disease protection. One drug candidate targets precancerous cervical cells, offering a safer treatment option. Two vaccine candidates will combat major bacterial gut infections. This initiative promotes government research into practical healthcare products.

items.title: Punjab & Sind Bank eyes Rs 4 lakh cr business by FY29: Lender bets big on technology transformation
items.summary: Punjab & Sind Bank is setting ambitious goals, aiming for a staggering ₹4 lakh crore in business by the fiscal year 2028-29. The bank projects a healthy credit growth ranging from eighteen to twenty percent for the current year. To bolster its growth, it is investing in digital transformation projects aimed at enhancing digital lending capabilities and account acquisition strategies.

items.title: Absolut vodka maker Pernod Ricard withdraws plea against $314 mn India tax demand
items.summary: French firm Pernod Ricard has withdrawn its court challenge against India's tax demand. The company faced a $314 million demand for undervaluing Scotch whisky imports. This dispute is the largest tax issue for Pernod in India. Pernod argued India did not provide sufficient investigation data during its probe. The company is also involved in other legal battles regarding liquor policy violations.

items.title: Fertiliser vessels stuck at ports, raise supply fears for peak kharif sowing
items.summary: Fertiliser vessels are stranded at Indian ports, hindering crop nutrient supplies.  Sowing has accelerated after rainfall revival, increasing demand for timely distribution.  Port congestion and supply chain issues are delaying crucial fertiliser dispatches.  West Asia crisis and rerouting of vessels contribute to these significant delays.  Industry executives express concerns over potential disruptions to the kharif sowing season.

items.title: Certus Capital invests Rs 275 crore in Gurgaon residential project
items.summary: Certus Capital has invested ₹275 crore in a Gurgaon low-rise residential project. This marks their second investment in the National Capital Region this year. The real estate private credit platform has deployed capital across several Indian cities. Certus Capital focuses on low-supply residential segments with strong end-user demand. The firm holds licenses as an Alternative Investment Fund and NBFC.

items.title: Tractor sales growth in FY27 to slow on weak monsoon fears: Report
items.summary: Tractor industry volume growth is expected to moderate to one to four percent this fiscal year. This moderation follows a significant 23.5% growth in the previous fiscal year. The Indian Meteorological Department forecasts a below-normal monsoon due to anticipated El Nino conditions. Recent rainfall data indicates precipitation deficits across several Indian regions. Prolonged rainfall shortfalls could negatively impact farm incomes and tractor demand.

items.title: Glenmark sees innovative products driving 70% revenue in 5-6 years: CMD Glenn Saldanha
items.summary: Glenmark anticipates innovative products will drive nearly seventy percent of its revenue.  This strategic shift moves the company away from its traditional generics-led model.  The company is developing next-generation products targeting oncology and other complex diseases.  Glenmark aims to become a global powerhouse in oncology within ten years.  Artificial intelligence will significantly accelerate drug discovery and development processes.

items.title: Man Tied to $100 Million NJ Deli Scam Gets 21 Months in Prison
items.summary: A twice-convicted felon who helped orchestrate a scheme to inflate the value of a small New Jersey delicatessen to more than $100 million was ordered to spend 21 months behind bars.

items.title: Inside IndiGo's succession plan: Rahul Bhatia prepares son Madhav for future leadership
items.summary: Rahul Bhatia has begun grooming his son Madhav Bhatia for a future leadership role at IndiGo, exposing him to executive meetings and mentoring under InterGlobe Enterprises CEO Aditya Pande. The move comes amid leadership changes and increased promoter oversight following last year's operational disruptions, as India's largest airline prepares for its next phase of global expansion.

items.title: Geopolitics biggest threat to FMCG: Wipro Consumer MD Kumar Chander
items.summary: Geopolitical tensions and rising crude oil prices are the primary concerns for India's consumer goods sector.  Demand is improving, supported by quick commerce and premiumization trends in urban areas.  Rural demand has also strengthened over the past six to nine months.  Wipro Consumer acquired S Brands, expanding its Southeast Asian presence significantly.  Input costs remain elevated, impacting margins despite some easing from recent peaks.

items.title: Shilpa Shetty-backed Bastian Hospitality in talks to raise capital for India, overseas expansion
items.summary: Bastian Hospitality is raising capital from investors for expansion plans. The fine-dining chain aims to sell a minority stake to support growth. They plan to diversify into bars, cafes, and social spaces. Bastian targets revenue of one thousand crore rupees by FY27. The company is also exploring international market opportunities.

items.title: FCNR(B): Data show banks have raised $20.7 bn under scheme; $10 bn more may flow in
items.summary: The optimism follows RBI's disclosure that banks have collectively garnered $20.7 billion under its special incentive window, a pace that has already eased earlier concerns the scheme was struggling for traction. Of the total, FCNR(B) deposits accounted for the largest share at $17.4 billion, mobilised within six weeks of the scheme becoming operational. This was followed by overseas foreign currency borrowings (OFCBs) at $2 billion and external commercial borrowings (ECBs) at $1.3 billion.

items.title: FCNR(B): Data show banks have raised $20.7 bn under scheme; $10 bn more may flow in

items.title: Ratings blackout may cast a shadow over brands' TV ad spends
items.summary: A prolonged suspension of BARC television ratings could disrupt festive-season advertising, with marketers warning that reality shows such as Kaun Banega Crorepati, Bigg Boss and India Ke Top 1% may be the worst affected.

items.title: FSSAI cracks down on misleading 'healthy food' claims, tightens noose on brands
items.summary: India's food regulator is targeting deceptive health claims made by beverage and food companies. Approximately eighty-five percent of digital advertisements fail to meet advertising standards. Brands are revising labels and packaging after receiving notices from the FSSAI. The health food market is expanding rapidly, attracting significant venture capital funding. Experts suggest clearer standards will encourage genuine product innovation and development.

items.title: Competition regulator dismisses case against Jindal Stainless over deals with Indonesian suppliers
items.summary: The Competition Commission of India dismissed a case against Jindal Stainless.  No evidence showed procurement arrangements violated antitrust rules.  The complaint alleged exclusive deals with Indonesian suppliers for crucial inputs.  The commission found no proof of restricted access for competitors.  Jindal Stainless's dealership program was deemed voluntary by the regulator.

items.title: Jana SFB reports ₹169-crore gap in FY25 gross NPAs
items.summary: Jana Small Finance Bank disclosed a ₹169 crore divergence in gross non-performing assets. The bank's reported GNPAs were lower than the Reserve Bank of India's assessment. This divergence led to an additional ₹42 crore provisioning for the bank. Consequently, the bank's net profit for the fiscal year was reduced. The RBI audit identified under-reported NPAs, necessitating corrections by banks.

items.title: Jana SFB reports ₹169-crore gap in FY25 gross NPAs
items.summary: Jana Small Finance Bank disclosed a ₹169 crore divergence in gross non-performing assets. The bank's reported GNPAs were lower than the Reserve Bank of India's assessment. items.title: Banks lure NRIs with high-return FCNR(B) deposits, warn of leverage risks
items.summary: Banks are attracting NRI dollar deposits through RBI's FCNR(B) window. These deposits offer projected returns of thirteen to sixteen percent. Customers can borrow multiple times their capital to amplify these dollar yields. However, early redemption carries substantial penalties and lender control over pledged funds. Investors face liquidity constraints and regulatory risks with these leveraged products.

items.title: Bira 91 beer founder Ankur Jain quits board, gives up control in settlement
items.summary: Bira 91 founder Ankur Jain has stepped down from the company's board. He is giving up all executive powers and ownership to settle disputes. The company faces a major financial crisis and has suspended production. Existing investors will recapitalize Bira 91 and resume operations soon. Legal proceedings between parties have also been dropped as part of the agreement.

items.title: Subsidy deadline may be extended for electric-two-wheelers; PM  E-Drive scheme needs more funds
items.summary: The Ministry of Heavy Industries is considering extending subsidies for domestically manufactured electric two-wheelers beyond the current July 31 deadline, although this would require additional budgetary support as the ₹10,900-cr PM E-Drive scheme has already committed all its allocated funds. The subsidy is expected to remain at around ₹5,000 per vehicle.

items.title: Registration of redevelopment agreement alone doesn’t trigger tax, rules ITAT
items.summary: Mumbai ITAT ruled agreement registration does not mean property receipt.  Tax cannot be levied on future property rights under anti-abuse provisions.  The tribunal allowed a taxpayer's appeal against a ₹1.38 crore tax addition.  Registration creates contractual rights, not actual property possession or receipt.  This decision clarifies tax treatment for ongoing redevelopment projects in Mumbai.

items.title: Airtel’s Fast Lane plan delivers faster downloads, lower latencies, shows analysis
items.summary: Bharti Airtel's FastLane premium postpaid service delivers faster download speeds, lower latency and significantly higher access to 5G standalone than standard users, according to OpenSignal. While Airtel says the feature improves network efficiency without affecting others, experts argue the findings could reignite concerns over network neutrality and prioritised access.

items.title: House panel seeks periodic review of competition law framework, cites repeated violations of rules
items.summary: A parliamentary panel recommended reviewing competition law for the digital sector.  It called for vigorous enforcement of monetary penalty guidelines by the CCI.  The panel also stressed a transparent methodology for calculating penalty amounts.  Adequate protection for small businesses from monopolies was also suggested.  Enhanced cooperation between the CCI and other regulators was emphasized.

items.title: Automobile industry urges formal mechanism for petrol pump fuel quality checks amid E20 concerns
items.summary: Automakers want a formal system to detect fuel contamination at petrol pumps.  Drivers report worsening vehicle performance after increased ethanol blending in petrol.  Investigations reveal elevated chloride and moisture levels in supplied petrol.  Industry executives cite infrastructure issues causing fuel contamination and phase separation.  They suggest an independent body, possibly BIS, should certify fuel quality at pumps.

items.title: IHCL Q1 net profit rises 21% to ₹358 crore
MCA plans unified digital platform for insolvency ecosystem, seeks stakeholders’ inputs
items.summary: The Ministry of Corporate Affairs has proposed creating a unified digital platform for India's insolvency ecosystem to speed up the resolution of stressed companies under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC).

items.title: Weak macroeconomic conditions will continue to constrain IT spend for another three years, says S&P’s Chi Yang Leong

items.title: India's renewable energy installed capacity reaches 288.58 GW from 76.38 GW in 2014: Shripad Naik
items.summary: Renewable energy capacity grew significantly from 2014 to 2026. Solar and wind power form the largest portions of this installed capacity. Foreign direct investment totaling USD 45.72 billion flowed into the sector. Domestic financial institutions also deployed substantial funds towards renewable energy projects. Non-fossil fuel sources now contribute significantly to the nation's electricity generation.

items.title: Bajaj Auto Q1 net profit surges 46% to ₹3,226 crore aided by volume and exports growth
items.summary: Bajaj Auto Ltd. witnessed double-digit growth on all fronts (ICE/EV, Domestic/Exports, 2W/3W), and the growth was sustained despite a challenging external environment

items.title: Jamie Dimon says AI spending will pay off—but 'definitely not' on the timeline you expect
items.summary: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon expresses cautious optimism about AI investments, predicting they will pay off in the long run but warns against complacency due to geopolitical and fiscal risks that could disrupt markets.

items.title: PFRDA opens on-tap registration for pension fund managers under NPS
items.summary: The move allows eligible firms to apply at any time to manage assets under the National Pension System and Unified Pension Scheme

items.title: WTO urges India to cut trade-restrictive measures reliance, New Delhi flags global non-tariff barriers
items.summary: World Trade Organization (WTO) Secretariat has urged India to improve its business environment and reduce reliance on high tariffs, import-export controls and other trade-restrictive measures to boost competitiveness and attract more foreign investment ahead of India's 8th Trade Policy Review.

items.title: Sunteck Realty Q1 profit jumps 26% on strong sales, margin growth
items.summary: Sunteck Realty reported a twenty-six percent profit increase for the first quarter.  Healthy residential sales and improved operating margins supported this growth.  The company's net profit rose to approximately forty-two crore rupees.  Customer collections also saw a seventeen percent year-on-year increase.  This performance indicates sustained demand in the premium housing segment.

items.title: Cube Highways Trust raises ₹1,687.5 crore from anchor investors ahead of InvIT IPO
items.summary: Premji Invest-backed Prazim Trading and Investment Co led the anchor book, with strong participation from insurers and mutual funds

items.title: Oil climbs as traders weigh slew of risks from Hormuz to Red Sea
items.summary: The global benchmark rose about 2% to around $91 a barrel as the US continued its 10th day of strikes on Iran

items.title: PLI schemes attract ₹2.4 lakh crore investments, generate over 14 lakh jobs till March
items.summary: Solar modules, pharmaceuticals and automobiles lead investments as cumulative exports cross ₹15.2 lakh crore

items.title: India-U.K. FTA creates predictability for businesses on both sides: City of London policy chief
items.summary: The India-U.K. trade deal came into effect on July 15, 2026. In a written interview with The Hindu, Chris Hayward, Policy Chairman of the City of London Corporation, the U.K.’s financial and commercial hub, discusses the implications of the agreement, including what it means for manufacturing, infrastructure and financial services, as well as how investors can navigate changes in U.K. political leadership.

items.title: TFCI Q1 profit doubles to ₹61 crore on higher interest and other income
items.summary: TFCI’s gross NPA ratio rose marginally to 0.41 per cent, while net NPAs remained nil due to full provisioning

items.title: Maruti Suzuki to increase prices from August as input costs rise
items.summary: With inflationary burdens now at elevated levels and the adverse cost environment continuing, the company is constrained to pass on a portion of the increased costs to the market, Maruti Suzuki India Ltd stated in a filing

items.title: After Hormuz, Gate of Tears may make the world cry. What about India?
items.summary: The Bab el-Mandeb Strait, a key shipping route connecting Red Sea and Gulf of Aden, faces growing risks of disruption after Yemen’s Houthi movement warned of a maritime blockade targeting Saudi shipping. With US-Iran tensions escalating, the possibility of a closure of the waterway has emerged as a major global trade and energy concern.

items.title: Gold, silver smuggling surges after Indian govt more than doubles import duty
items.summary: Data provided in the Lok Sabha show over 186% rise in gold and 10% increase in silver

items.title: Adani Total Gas Q1 FY27 profit declines 14% to ₹142 cr
items.summary: The company’s revenue from operations, however, increased 27 per cent year-on-year to ₹1,907 crore during the period

items.summary: The company posted a net profit of ₹1,237 crore in the first quarter.

items.title: Indian bonds come under pressure as crude prices surge over $90 a barrel
items.summary: According to available data, the one-year swap ended 1.75% bps higher at 5.9675%, the two-year rose 2.5% bps to 6.1375% and three years bond jumped 3.5% bps to 6.44%

items.summary: Spread across 30,000 sq. ft. (2,800 sq. mt.); the second city store will open at DLF Avenue at Saket in New Delhi and cater to consumers in South and Central Delhi among others

items.summary: Crop growth is satisfactory under the prevailing field conditions and desirable rains, says its ED, DN Pathak

items.title: Mahindra Finance Q1 profit jumps 75% to  ₹927 crore on higher interest margin, lower provisions
items.summary: Mahindra and Mahindra Financial Services on Tuesday reported a 75% jump in its consolidated net profit at  ₹927 crore in the June quarter of FY27 on higher net interest margins and lower provisions.&nbsp;

items.title: Mahindra Finance Q1 PAT jumps 70% to ₹899 crore as AUM rises 13%
items.summary: Disbursements increased 22% to ₹15,564 crore from ₹12,808 crore a year earlierMahindra Finance’s profit surged 70 per cent to ₹899 crore in the first quarter as AUM grew 13 per cent to ₹1.37 lakh crore, with Chairman Anish Shah’s FY26 message highlighting the tighter risk controls, digital transformation and diversification strategy supporting the company’s next phase of growth.

items.title: SC quashes case against Standard Chartered Bank over violations of FERA provisions
items.summary: The Supreme Court quashed a 23-year-old criminal complaint against Standard Chartered Bank.  This decision provided relief to the bank after a lengthy legal process.  The court cited a lack of documentary evidence supporting the complaint.  It also noted the trial had not progressed beyond serving summons.  The Supreme Court's inherent powers were exercised to end these proceedings.

items.title: TVS Motor, Bajaj Auto post record Q1 profits on EV and export growth
items.summary: TVS Motor revenue grows 38% to ₹13,896 crore while Bajaj Auto’s revenue rises 37% to ₹17,244 crore.

items.title: Bandhan Bank Q1 net up 35% at ₹ 501.67 crore as provisions fall 40.5%
items.summary: During the first quarter this fiscal, the bank’s NII rose 5.92% y-o-y at ₹2,920.58 crore from ₹2,757.24 crore for the corresponding period last fiscal

items.title: Ramco Cements crosses ₹1000 crore in disposal of non-core assets in FY26
items.summary: Ramco also expects their emerging business segment of the Construction Chemicals business to clock ₹2,000 crores of revenue over the next five years

items.summary: The integrated mining and logistics services provider had earlier raised ₹135 crore from anchor investors

items.title: TVS, Bajaj report record quarterly revenue, see demand momentum continuing
items.summary: Both companies attributed their strong performance to sustained domestic demand following GST cuts and robust export growth, which helped offset higher fuel prices and logistics disruptions caused by the West Asia conflict.

items.title: Mahindra Finance Q1 Results: Profit jumps 75% to Rs 927 crore on higher interest margin, lower provisions
items.summary: Mahindra and Mahindra Financial Services has experienced a remarkable seventy-five percent surge in net profit, driven by enhanced net interest margins and reduced provisions. The chief executive highlighted the positive impact of a recent rights issuance on the cost of funding. Disbursements soared by twenty-two percent, and there was notable growth in the non-wheels sector. However, a minor rise in overdue accounts necessitated strategic adjustments within certain segments.

items.title: Privatise all CPSEs; bring oil, gas, alcohol under GST: US think tank wishlist for India
items.summary: A US think tank proposed thirty reforms to boost India's economic growth.  These suggestions include a ten-year plan for privatizing all CPSEs.  The government has already completed three of these recommended reforms.  Difficult reforms involve land acquisition and bringing more sectors under GST.  Further initiatives could accelerate India's global competitiveness.

items.title: Q1 Results Today Highlights: ATGL Q1 PAT down 14%, Adani Energy PAT zooms, TVS Motor, Bajaj Auto & Granules India profit rise
items.summary: Q1 Results Today, 21st July 2026 Highlights

items.title: Tamil Nadu expands share in India’s exports over the past three years
items.summary: Tamil Nadu has been the third largest exporting State in India for three consecutive financial years.

items.summary: India's debt-to-GDP ratio moderated to 58.2 percent in FY26, showing improved fiscal health.
